source_pr.title: *: fix timestamp conversion
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/36707

Problem Summary:

This PR fixes inaccurate timestamp conversion algorithms that appeared in various packages.

From the perspective of dimensional analysis, the following algorithm does not hold:

```go
time.Now().UnixNano() / int64(time.Millisecond)
```

> The above calculation doesn't really make sense. You don't divide nanoseconds by miliseconds. It happens to be the case that golang choses to represent times down to nanosecond and the constant 'Millisecond' is 1,000,000. Mathematically speaking, calculation should be: time.Now().UnixNano() * (time.Nanosecond / time.Millisecond).

Reference:  https://stackoverflow.com/a/24122933

I've scanned the code base and implemented a corrected version based on [time.UnixMilli](https://cs.opensource.google/go/go/+/go1.18.4:src/time/time.go;l=1349) and [time.UnixMicro](https://cs.opensource.google/go/go/+/go1.18.4:src/time/time.go;l=1355), which were introduced to the standard library in go 1.17.

Note that [util.TSOToRoughTime](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/master/util/tso.go) is intentionally unmodified.

### What is changed and how it works?

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```
